svg.title: NKW-K5-00-1 Power Distribution — 1977 Chevrolet K5 Blazer / LS3 / Motec M130 / PDM30
svg.desc: Section 0: Power Distribution Diagram. Complete current flow from battery to every load. ANSI D landscape (22x34 inches). Drawing NKW-K5-00-1 Rev --.
svg.text: 1977 Chevrolet K5 Blazer — LS3 6.2L / Motec M130 / PDM30
Complete current flow: Battery → Protection → Distribution → Every Load
Solid-state protection: auto-retry, current limiting, thermal shutdown
CAN: B25/B26 to M130 B17/B18 (500 kbps)
SHARED OUTPUT CIRCUITS (spliced from PDM channels)
GROUND BUS — SEE SEC 12 (GROUND DISTRIBUTION) FOR COMPLETE DETAIL
ALTERNATOR: 220A OUTPUT vs 135A CONTINUOUS = 85A MARGIN (63% utilization)
1. PDM30 provides solid-state protection: no fuses to replace. Channels auto-retry
after thermal shutdown. Current limiting protects wiring at channel rating.
2. 20A channels use dual-pin outputs (two connector pins paralleled) for current
capacity. BOTH pins must be wired. Single-pin = 10A max.
3. Non-PDM circuits (starter, fuel pump, iBooster, amplifier) require conventional
protection: fusible links, circuit breakers, or relays as shown.
4. Battery disconnect (Motec RBD 190) interrupts ALL circuits except starter.
Starter relay is upstream of disconnect for emergency starting.
5. CAN bus (M130 B17/B18 to PDM30 B25/B26): 22AWG twisted pair, 120 ohm
termination at each end. PDM channels controlled via CAN, not hardwired switches.
